channel.title: Dism on ErrorCode — Error Code Reference & Solutions
channel.description: Recent content in Dism on ErrorCode — Error Code Reference & Solutions

item.title: [Solution] DLL Is Corrupt or Missing — Fix Corrupted DLL Files
item.description:
DLL Is Corrupt or Missing

A corrupt DLL file produces errors ranging from “file is corrupt” messages to application crashes and blue screens. Windows may report:

“Windows cannot verify the digital signature for this file.”

Or:

“The file [filename].dll is corrupt.”

What This Error Means

DLL files can become corrupted due to disk errors, interrupted writes, malware, or failed Windows updates. A corrupted DLL may have valid file size but contain garbage data, causing the loader to reject it or the program to crash when it tries to use the corrupted functions.

item.title: [Solution] Windows Update Error 0x80073712 Component Store Fix
item.description:
[Solution] Windows Update Error 0x80073712 Component Store Fix

Error 0x80073712 is a Windows Update error that indicates the component store is corrupted. The full error message is ERROR_WU_E_UNEXPECTED_UPDATE_ENGINE_ERROR or CBS_E_STORE_CORRUPTION. Windows cannot proceed with updates because the Component-Based Servicing (CBS) database is damaged or contains inconsistent data.

This error affects both Windows 10 and 11 and typically blocks cumulative and feature updates from installing. The system cannot reconcile the expected component state with what is actually present on disk.

item.title: [Solution] Error 0x80070490 Windows 11/10 — Element Not Found Fix
item.description:
[Solution] Error 0x80070490 Windows 11/10 — Element Not Found Fix

Error 0x80070490 is a Windows Update error with the message Element not found. It occurs when the Windows Update agent or servicing stack cannot locate a required component, preventing updates from being installed.

This error affects both Windows 10 and 11 and commonly blocks cumulative updates, security patches, and feature upgrades.

Description

The full error message typically reads:

“There were problems installing some updates, but we’ll try again later. Error 0x80070490 — Element not found”

item.title: [Solution] Error 0x800F081F Windows 11/10 — Source Files Not Found Fix
item.description:
[Solution] Error 0x800F081F Windows 11/10 — Source Files Not Found Fix

Error 0x800F081F occurs when Windows cannot find the source files required to install a feature, most commonly the .NET Framework 3.5. It affects both Windows 10 and 11 when enabling legacy features.

This error typically blocks installation of .NET Framework 3.5, which many older applications and games require. The fix involves pointing Windows to valid installation media or enabling the feature through alternative methods.
